language.title: Language
language.description: Desktop-wide UI language. Applies instantly — no restart needed.
language.updateFailed: Could not save the language preference

telemetry.title: Pipeline Telemetry
telemetry.description: Capture token usage, phase durations, and subagent activity for diagnostic export. Off by default.
telemetry.toggleDescription: When on, OTEL data from pipeline jobs is captured locally. Use the <mono>Export diagnostic</mono> button on any job card to download.

prePrompt.title: Rail Pre-prompt
prePrompt.description: Extra project-specific instructions appended to implement and batch-implement rail jobs after the ticket context and before execution.
prePrompt.label: Pre-prompt
prePrompt.placeholder: Example: Prefer incremental changes, keep migrations backward compatible, and add tests for every rail change.
prePrompt.helper: Use this for stable project guidance that should accompany every rail implementation run.

ultraPrePrompt.title: Ultracode pre-prompt
ultraPrePrompt.description: Instruction sent to Claude in Ultracode (Claude-only rails). Ultracode skips the OpenSpec pipeline — it hands Claude this pre-prompt plus the spec text and lets it implement autonomously. Leave blank to use the built-in default.
ultraPrePrompt.label: Ultra pre-prompt
ultraPrePrompt.placeholder: Leave blank to use the default Ultracode instruction.
ultraPrePrompt.helper: The spec text is appended automatically after this pre-prompt. Empty = default.

budget.title: Budget
budget.description: Set a daily spend cap for this project. The queue auto-pauses when the limit is hit.
budget.dailyHelper: Leave blank to disable. Spend is calculated over the last 24 hours.
budget.perJobHelper: Alert when a single job in this project exceeds this amount.
budget.invalidNumber: Enter a positive number or leave blank to disable

desktop.title: Desktop Settings
desktop.description: Manage registered projects and view Desktop information.
desktop.techUrlDescription: Base URL for the specrails-tech API (default: http://localhost:3000)
desktop.dailyBudgetHelper: Global daily spend limit across all projects. Queues auto-pause when exceeded.
desktop.perJobHelper: Alert when a single job exceeds this amount. Leave blank to disable.
desktop.dailyBudgetSaveFailed: Failed to save Desktop daily budget
desktop.platformTourDescription: Replay the welcome wizard to review key features.

notifications.heading: OS Notifications
notifications.description: Show native desktop notifications when jobs complete or fail. Notifications only appear when the tab is not focused.

webhooks.heading: Outbound Webhooks
webhooks.description: Notify external tools (Slack, Zapier, CI/CD) on Desktop events. Requests are signed via X-Specrails-Signature when a secret is set.

terminal.title: Terminal panel
terminal.desktopDescription: Desktop-wide defaults applied to every project unless a per-project override is set.
terminal.projectDescription: Per-project overrides for the terminal panel. Leave a field unchanged to inherit the Desktop default.
terminal.quickScript: Quick script (pasted into active terminal — Enter manually)

codeSection.heading: Code section
codeSection.budgetHelper: Cap on `surface=file-summary` spend per calendar month. User-initiated regenerations can override.

mobile.heading: Mobile companion
mobile.description: Control Specrails from the SpecRails Companion app on your phone, 100% over your local network. Off by default.
mobile.resetConfirm: Reset the mobile identity? Every paired device will be revoked and must pair again.
mobile.windowsFirewall: Windows Firewall will ask to allow the SpecRails server on first enable — choose “Allow on private networks”.

pairDevice.title: Pair a device
pairDevice.description: Open SpecRails Companion on your phone and scan this code. It only works while this dialog is open.
